era_context: The late Augustan period; Augustus is consolidating his power and increasingly concerned with succession. The Western Han dynasty in China is in decline. The Parthian Empire is stable under Orodes II. In India, the Satavahana dynasty is expanding. This is a period of relative peace in the Mediterranean world.

events.title: Drusus the Younger's Rhine campaigns continue
events.description: Drusus the Younger continued his military campaigns against Germanic tribes along the Rhine frontier. These campaigns were part of Augustus's strategy to expand Roman control in northern Europe and secure the frontier. Drusus's military successes made him a prominent figure in Rome.
events.certainty_note: Drusus's campaigns on the Rhine are well-attested, but the exact chronology is somewhat uncertain.
cross_references: Part of broader Rhine campaign narrative (-12 to -9 BCE)

events.title: Augustus's consolidation of power
events.description: Augustus continued to consolidate his power and manage the succession crisis. The aging emperor was increasingly concerned with ensuring a stable transition of power. This period saw intensive political maneuvering among potential heirs and their supporters.
events.certainty_note: Augustus's political activities are well-documented in later sources, but specific events for -13 are not distinctly recorded.

events.title: Western Han Dynasty political instability
events.description: The Western Han court under Emperor Cheng continued to be marked by factional disputes and the growing influence of eunuchs and the imperial family's maternal relatives. This period saw increasing corruption and the weakening of central authority.
events.certainty_note: The general political situation is well-documented, but specific events for -13 are not distinctly recorded.

disconfirming_evidence: No significant disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. The characterization of Drusus's campaigns as successful is generally accepted, though some modern scholars emphasize the limitations and costs of these campaigns.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 13 BCE is dominated by Roman sources, particularly later compilations. Chinese sources provide information about Han court politics but are similarly retrospective. There is a significant bias toward political and military history; economic and social history are underrepresented. The perspective is largely that of the elite.

graph_edges.note: Drusus's Rhine campaigns continued until his death in 9 BCE
